parameters.formDescription: Select what you want to check. The workflow reads approved data from Supabase tables, generates a chart, writes an executive summary, and emails the insight snapshot.

values.option: Which category of customer inquiries causes most problems?

conditions.rightValue: Which category of customer inquiries causes most problems?

assignments.name: chart_config

messageValues.message: You are an executive business analyst.  Your job is to turn structured KPI results into a short, manager-friendly insight.  Rules: - Be concise and clear. - Do not over-explain. - Do not invent numbers. - Use only the provided data. - Mention whether the result is good, bad, or needs attention. - Write for a non-technical manager. - Output in clean HTML for email/Teams.

parameters.subject: InsightFlow Supabase - No Data Found

assignments.name: insight
assignments.value: =We could not match your selection to an available analysis. You selected Area: "{{ $('On form submission').item.json['Analysis Area'] }}", Question: "{{ $('On form submission').item.json.Question }}", Time Period: "{{ $('On form submission').item.json['Time Period'] }}". Please choose one of the listed options and try again.

parameters.query:
WITH params AS (
  SELECT '{{ $('On form submission').item.json['Time Period'] }}'::text AS time_period
),
ref AS (
  SELECT max(sale_date)::date AS ref_date
  FROM public.business_revenue
),
periods AS (
  SELECT
    p.time_period,
    r.ref_date,
    CASE
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Yesterday' THEN r.ref_date
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last Week' THEN r.ref_date - 6
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last month' THEN r.ref_date - 29
      ELSE r.ref_date
    END::date AS current_start,
    r.ref_date::date AS current_end,
    CASE
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Yesterday' THEN r.ref_date - 1
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last Week' THEN r.ref_date - 13
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last month' THEN r.ref_date - 59
      ELSE (
        SELECT max(sale_date)::date
        FROM public.business_revenue
        WHERE sale_date < r.ref_date
      )
    END::date AS previous_start,
    CASE
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Yesterday' THEN r.ref_date - 1
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last Week' THEN r.ref_date - 7
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last month' THEN r.ref_date - 30
      ELSE (
        SELECT max(sale_date)::date
        FROM public.business_revenue
        WHERE sale_date < r.ref_date
      )
    END::date AS previous_end,
    CASE
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Yesterday' THEN 'the latest available day vs. the previous day'
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last Week' THEN 'the latest 7 days vs. the previous 7 days'
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last month' THEN 'the latest 30 days vs. the previous 30 days'
      ELSE 'the two most recent dates in the database'
    END AS comparison_basis
  FROM params p
  CROSS JOIN ref r
),
sums AS (
  SELECT
    p.*,
    COALESCE((
      SELECT sum(revenue)
      FROM public.business_revenue
      WHERE sale_date BETWEEN p.current_start AND p.current_end
    ), 0)::numeric AS latest_revenue,
    COALESCE((
      SELECT sum(revenue)
      FROM public.business_revenue
      WHERE sale_date BETWEEN p.previous_start AND p.previous_end
    ), 0)::numeric AS previous_revenue
  FROM periods p
),
calc AS (
  SELECT
    *,
    latest_revenue - previous_revenue AS change,
    CASE
      WHEN latest_revenue - previous_revenue > 0 THEN 'increased'
      WHEN latest_revenue - previous_revenue < 0 THEN 'decreased'
      ELSE 'stayed flat'
    END AS direction,
    CASE
      WHEN previous_revenue = 0 THEN NULL
      ELSE round(((latest_revenue - previous_revenue) / previous_revenue) * 100, 2)
    END AS percentage_change
  FROM sums
),
daily AS (
  SELECT
    br.sale_date,
    sum(br.revenue)::numeric AS total_revenue
  FROM public.business_revenue br
  CROSS JOIN calc c
  WHERE
    c.ref_date IS NOT NULL
    AND c.previous_start IS NOT NULL
    AND br.sale_date BETWEEN c.previous_start AND c.current_end
  GROUP BY br.sale_date
)
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN c.ref_date IS NULL THEN 'no_data'
    WHEN c.previous_start IS NULL THEN 'not_enough_data'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS status,
  'business_revenue' AS source_table,
  'Revenue change' AS analysis,
  'Revenue Trend' AS title,
  'line' AS chart_type,
  'Revenue' AS metric_label,
  c.time_period,
  CASE
    WHEN c.ref_date IS NULL THEN
      'No revenue data was found in the business_revenue table.'
    WHEN c.previous_start IS NULL THEN
      'Not enough revenue dates were found to compare revenue.'
    WHEN c.percentage_change IS NULL THEN
      'Revenue ' || c.direction || ' comparing ' || c.comparison_basis || '. Previous revenue was 0, so percentage change is not available.'
    ELSE
      'Revenue ' || c.direction || ' by ' || c.percentage_change || '% comparing ' || c.comparison_basis || '.'
  END AS insight,
  CASE
    WHEN c.ref_date IS NULL OR c.previous_start IS NULL THEN '[]'::jsonb
    ELSE COALESCE(to_jsonb(array_agg(to_char(d.sale_date, 'YYYY-MM-DD') ORDER BY d.sale_date)), '[]'::jsonb)
  END AS chart_labels,
  CASE
    WHEN c.ref_date IS NULL OR c.previous_start IS NULL THEN '[]'::jsonb
    ELSE COALESCE(to_jsonb(array_agg(d.total_revenue ORDER BY d.sale_date)), '[]'::jsonb)
  END AS chart_values,
  jsonb_build_object(
    'comparison_basis', c.comparison_basis,
    'previous_revenue', c.previous_revenue,
    'latest_revenue', c.latest_revenue,
    'change', c.change,
    'percentage_change', c.percentage_change,
    'direction', c.direction
  ) AS ranking
FROM calc c
LEFT JOIN daily d ON true
GROUP BY
  c.ref_date,
  c.previous_start,
  c.time_period,
  c.comparison_basis,
  c.previous_revenue,
  c.latest_revenue,
  c.change,
  c.percentage_change,
  c.direction;

WITH params AS (
  SELECT '{{ $('On form submission').item.json['Time Period'] }}'::text AS time_period
),
ref AS (
  SELECT max(sale_date)::date AS ref_date
  FROM public.business_revenue
),
periods AS (
  SELECT
    p.time_period,
    r.ref_date,
    CASE
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Yesterday' THEN r.ref_date
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last Week' THEN r.ref_date - 6
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last month' THEN r.ref_date - 29
      ELSE NULL
    END::date AS start_date,
    CASE
      WHEN p.time_period IN ('Yesterday', 'Last Week', 'Last month') THEN r.ref_date
      ELSE NULL
    END::date AS end_date
  FROM params p
  CROSS JOIN ref r
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT
    br.country,
    round(sum(br.revenue), 2)::numeric AS revenue
  FROM public.business_revenue br
  CROSS JOIN periods p
  WHERE
    p.time_period = 'All data'
    OR br.sale_date BETWEEN p.start_date AND p.end_date
  GROUP BY br.country
),
ordered_ranked AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM ranked
  ORDER BY revenue DESC, country ASC
),
agg AS (
  SELECT
    count(*) AS row_count,
    COALESCE(to_jsonb(array_agg(country ORDER BY revenue DESC, country ASC)), '[]'::jsonb) AS labels,
    COALESCE(to_jsonb(array_agg(revenue ORDER BY revenue DESC, country ASC)), '[]'::jsonb) AS values,
    COALESCE(
      jsonb_agg(
        jsonb_build_object(
          'country', country,
          'revenue', revenue
        )
        ORDER BY revenue DESC, country ASC
      ),
      '[]'::jsonb
    ) AS ranking
  FROM ordered_ranked
),
top_row AS (
  SELECT country, revenue
  FROM ordered_ranked
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN a.row_count = 0 THEN 'no_data' ELSE NULL END AS status,
  'business_revenue' AS source_table,
  'Top revenue country' AS analysis,
  'Revenue by Country' AS title,
  'bar' AS chart_type,
  'Revenue' AS metric_label,
  p.time_period,
  CASE
    WHEN a.row_count = 0 THEN
      'No revenue data found for the selected time period in the business_revenue table.'
    ELSE
      t.country || ' generated the highest revenue with ' || t.revenue || ' (' || p.time_period || ').'
  END AS insight,
  a.labels AS chart_labels,
  a.values AS chart_values,
  a.ranking
FROM periods p
CROSS JOIN agg a
LEFT JOIN top_row t ON true;

WITH params AS (
  SELECT '{{ $('On form submission').item.json['Time Period'] }}'::text AS time_period
),
ref AS (
  SELECT max(ticket_date)::date AS ref_date
  FROM public.customer_support_tickets
),
periods AS (
  SELECT
    p.time_period,
    r.ref_date,
    CASE
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Yesterday' THEN r.ref_date
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last Week' THEN r.ref_date - 6
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last month' THEN r.ref_date - 29
      ELSE NULL
    END::date AS start_date,
    CASE
      WHEN p.time_period IN ('Yesterday', 'Last Week', 'Last month') THEN r.ref_date
      ELSE NULL
    END::date AS end_date
  FROM params p
  CROSS JOIN ref r
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT
    cst.country,
    round(avg(cst.rating), 2)::numeric AS average_rating,
    count(*)::int AS rated_tickets
  FROM public.customer_support_tickets cst
  CROSS JOIN periods p
  WHERE
    cst.rating IS NOT NULL
    AND (
      p.time_period = 'All data'
      OR cst.ticket_date BETWEEN p.start_date AND p.end_date
    )
  GROUP BY cst.country
),
ordered_ranked AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM ranked
  ORDER BY average_rating ASC, rated_tickets DESC, country ASC
),
agg AS (
  SELECT
    count(*) AS row_count,
    COALESCE(to_jsonb(array_agg(country ORDER BY average_rating ASC, rated_tickets DESC, country ASC)), '[]'::jsonb) AS labels,
    COALESCE(to_jsonb(array_agg(average_rating ORDER BY average_rating ASC, rated_tickets DESC, country ASC)), '[]'::jsonb) AS values,
    COALESCE(
      jsonb_agg(
        jsonb_build_object(
          'country', country,
          'average_rating', average_rating,
          'rated_tickets', rated_tickets
        )
        ORDER BY average_rating ASC, rated_tickets DESC, country ASC
      ),
      '[]'::jsonb
    ) AS ranking
  FROM ordered_ranked
),
bottom_row AS (
  SELECT country, average_rating
  FROM ordered_ranked
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN a.row_count = 0 THEN 'no_data' ELSE NULL END AS status,
  'customer_support_tickets' AS source_table,
  'Customer satisfaction by country' AS analysis,
  'Average Rating by Country' AS title,
  'bar' AS chart_type,
  'Average Rating' AS metric_label,
  p.time_period,
  CASE
    WHEN a.row_count = 0 THEN
      'No customer ratings found for the selected time period in the customer_support_tickets table.'
    ELSE
      b.country || ' has the lowest customer satisfaction with an average rating of ' || b.average_rating || ' (' || p.time_period || ').'
  END AS insight,
  a.labels AS chart_labels,
  a.values AS chart_values,
  a.ranking
FROM periods p
CROSS JOIN agg a
LEFT JOIN bottom_row b ON true;

WITH params AS (
  SELECT '{{ $('On form submission').item.json['Time Period'] }}'::text AS time_period
),
ref AS (
  SELECT max(ticket_date)::date AS ref_date
  FROM public.customer_support_tickets
),
periods AS (
  SELECT
    p.time_period,
    r.ref_date,
    CASE
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Yesterday' THEN r.ref_date
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last Week' THEN r.ref_date - 6
      WHEN p.time_period = 'Last month' THEN r.ref_date - 29
      ELSE NULL
    END::date AS start_date,
    CASE
      WHEN p.time_period IN ('Yesterday', 'Last Week', 'Last month') THEN r.ref_date
      ELSE NULL
    END::date AS end_date
  FROM params p
  CROSS JOIN ref r
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT
    cst.category,
    count(*)::int AS tickets,
    count(*) FILTER (WHERE cst.status IN ('Open', 'In Progress'))::int AS unresolved,
    count(*) FILTER (WHERE cst.priority IN ('High', 'Critical'))::int AS serious_priority,
    (
      count(*)
      + count(*) FILTER (WHERE cst.status IN ('Open', 'In Progress'))
      + count(*) FILTER (WHERE cst.priority IN ('High', 'Critical'))
    )::int AS problem_score
  FROM public.customer_support_tickets cst
  CROSS JOIN periods p
  WHERE
    p.time_period = 'All data'
    OR cst.ticket_date BETWEEN p.start_date AND p.end_date
  GROUP BY cst.category
),
ordered_ranked AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM ranked
  ORDER BY problem_score DESC, unresolved DESC, serious_priority DESC, category ASC
),
agg AS (
  SELECT
    count(*) AS row_count,
    COALESCE(to_jsonb(array_agg(category ORDER BY problem_score DESC, unresolved DESC, serious_priority DESC, category ASC)), '[]'::jsonb) AS labels,
    COALESCE(to_jsonb(array_agg(problem_score ORDER BY problem_score DESC, unresolved DESC, serious_priority DESC, category ASC)), '[]'::jsonb) AS values,
    COALESCE(
      jsonb_agg(
        jsonb_build_object(
          'category', category,
          'tickets', tickets,
          'unresolved', unresolved,
          'serious_priority', serious_priority,
          'problem_score', problem_score
        )
        ORDER BY problem_score DESC, unresolved DESC, serious_priority DESC, category ASC
      ),
      '[]'::jsonb
    ) AS ranking
  FROM ordered_ranked
),
top_row AS (
  SELECT category, problem_score
  FROM ordered_ranked
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN a.row_count = 0 THEN 'no_data' ELSE NULL END AS status,
  'customer_support_tickets' AS source_table,
  'Problem categories' AS analysis,
  'Customer Support Problem Areas' AS title,
  'bar' AS chart_type,
  'Problem Score' AS metric_label,
  p.time_period,
  CASE
    WHEN a.row_count = 0 THEN
      'No customer support data found for the selected time period in the customer_support_tickets table.'
    ELSE
      t.category || ' appears to be the biggest support problem area with a problem score of ' || t.problem_score || ' (' || p.time_period || ').'
  END AS insight,
  a.labels AS chart_labels,
  a.values AS chart_values,
  a.ranking
FROM periods p
CROSS JOIN agg a
LEFT JOIN top_row t ON true;

parameters.content: Runs predefined SQL queries against Supabase/Postgres tables. Aggregation happens in SQL, not in n8n code.

User Input

Collects the analysis area, business question, time period, and recipient email.

Question Routing

Routes the request to the correct SQL analysis based on the selected area and question.

Chart Preparation

Transforms the SQL result into a QuickChart-compatible chart configuration.

AI Executive Summary

Uses OpenAI to turn the structured KPI result into a concise manager-friendly insight.

Email Delivery

Sends the chart and executive summary to the selected recipient.

Executive Insight Flow 

This workflow automates the analysis of executive KPIs from Supabase data. A user selects a business question via a form; the workflow runs an approved SQL query, generates a chart, creates an AI-generated executive summary, and emails the results.
